methods.method_name: Null Model Analyses of Species Co-occurrence
methods.description: This method involves analyzing species co-occurrence patterns in replicated local assemblages to determine if they differ from random chance, which can indicate species interactions, environmental filtering, or dispersal limitation.

steps.description: Organize the dataset into a binary presence-absence matrix where rows are taxa, columns are sites or samples, and entries indicate the presence (1) or absence (0) of a particular species in a site.
steps.searchable_summary: create binary presence-absence matrix from species data

methods.description: This method uses fossil pollen data from the late Quaternary of eastern North America to analyze vegetation composition and infer ecological responses to millennial-scale climate change.

steps.description: Collect fossil pollen data from the Neotoma Paleoecology Database with revised and standardized site age models.
steps.searchable_summary: download fossil pollen data from Neotoma Paleoecology Database

steps.description: Aggregate species-level data to the generic level and calculate relative abundance of each fossil pollen taxon at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: aggregate species data to genus level and calculate relative abundance

steps.description: Linearly interpolate relative abundances to 1000-year time steps from 21 kyr BP to the present.
Convert relative abundance data to presence-absence format for analysis with the program Pairs.
steps.searchable_summary: convert abundance data to presence-absence format

methods.description: This method quantifies environmental characteristics at each site using paleoclimate simulations and transforms climate variables to principal components for analysis.

steps.description: Extract paleoclimate simulation data from the National Center for Atmospheric Research Community Climate System Model ver. 3 (CCSM3).
steps.searchable_summary: download paleoclimate simulation data from CCSM3

steps.description: Downscale climate data to a 0.5 x 0.5 degree grid for every 1000 years from 21 kyr BP to the present.
steps.searchable_summary: downscale climate data to 0.5 degree grid

steps.description: Extract mean annual precipitation, mean winter temperature, and mean summer temperature for each site and time slice.
steps.searchable_summary: extract climate variables for each site and time slice

steps.description: Scale and center climate variables, then transform them to principal components axes.
